---
title: "fairpaprika Model Card"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{fairpaprika Model Card}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

## Overview

This vignette summarizes the governance/configuration aspects of `fairpaprika`. The canonical, more detailed model card lives in `inst/model_card.md`. Use that file for deployment and publication; this vignette provides a quick orientation.

## Core Assumptions

- Additive baseline utilities with monotone level increments; optional sparse interactions (selected pairs).
- Anchors: worst level = 0; normalization on top levels.
- Response model: `tau_equal` governs A/B/E bins; `eps_strict` is the constraint margin.
- Eligibility/dealbreakers are hard filters.

## Configuration Schema (high level)

- **domains**: criteria with ordered levels.
- **interactions**: `enabled`, `pairs`, `max_pairs`, `max_fraction`, `max_abs`, `relevance_tol`, activation triggers.
- **selector**: sampling (`n_samples`, `burnin`, `thin`), scoring weights (`alpha`–`eta`), `top_k`, `candidate_pool`, `pair_cap`, `eig_target`, `utility_top_k`.
- **fair**: `pair_coverage`, `exposure_balance`, `exposure_gap_limit`, `interaction_coverage`.
- **stop**: winner/top3 confidence, IG/no-progress thresholds, weight-span.
- **slack**: `enabled`, `penalty`, `warn_sum`, `warn_max`.
- **eligibility**: allowed/max levels, excluded profiles.

## Procedural Justice & Logging

- Per-question audit: type, crit_pair, IG, fairness, cost, implied, interaction flags, seed, balance relaxation.
- Run-level audit: coverage/exposure curves, interaction coverage, dominance rate, order sensitivity, slack stats.
- Procedural-justice report: coverage/exposure, family mix, burden metrics; exportable JSON/TXT.

## Explanations

- Top-3 explanations include drivers, against-arguments, robustness label, counterfactual criteria, interaction relevance, eligibility reasons.
- Interaction summary marks whether additive suffices (`relevance_tol`).

## Sanity Checks

- Dominance sanity: flags if a dominated option has higher utility.
- Interaction magnitude sanity: warns if `max_abs` exceeded.
- Eligibility and monotonicity enforced as hard constraints.

## Benchmarks

- `benchmark_ablation()` simulates synthetic patients to compare configs (Top-1/Top-3, questions, coverage) with optional noise.

## Privacy & Governance Guidance

- Store only necessary audit data; separate anonymized session logs from any clinical notes.
- Seeds/randomization recorded for reproducibility; avoid PII.
- When deploying, bundle or reference `inst/model_card.md` for full governance details.
